text:
There was once a little old lady who lived alone in the forest. She had quite a bit of money stashed away in a little coffeepot, which she never spent. One day a raccoon came up to her mug and asked, "Mama mia! Ever stop to taste that?"

The old lady smiled. "Only occasionally."

The raccoon nibbled a bit. "Oh dear, it's delicious!" he said. "Come on! I'll give you some of mine."

The old lady just shook her head. "Sorry, my dear. That's the last thing I indulge in."

Just then, a woodpecker swooped in. "Want some?" it said.

The raccoon did. The woodpecker drummed on a large walnut. "Aha! That'll keep you going all winter."

They chatted all morning, chatting about everything. That's how the raccoon learned all about the little old lady's hobbies and interests and how much
